Article Title: PM Modi announces fast-track courts for paper leak cases; Rahul, CJP hit back: 10 developments
Article Text:
PM Modi said the Centre would establish fast-track courts to ensure speedy trials and strict punishment in paper leak cases

Here are the 10 key developments:

PM Modi announces fast-track courts

Rahul Gandhi

says PM 'harmed the future of our youth'

PM will ensure that no culprit is spared: BJP President

Protests disrupt Parliament

Delhi HC to hear plea seeking NIA probe into ‘Sansad Chalo’ protest

16 Delhi Metro stations shut amid

CJP

protests

Delhi Police confirms personnel were targeted during CJP protest after viral CCTV footage

Dharmendra Pradhan must resign: CJP

Clashes erupt at Jantar Mantar, six police personnel injured

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday announced that the Centre will set up fast-track courts to ensure "swift and stringent punishment" for those involved in examination paper leaks.In a post on X, PM Modi said the government had directed the concerned authorities to take all necessary steps to safeguard students' interests, asserting that those responsible for compromising the future of the country's youth "will not be spared".The announcement triggered sharp response from the opposition with Congress dismissing it as inadequate and reiterating its demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.PM Modi said the Centre would establish fast-track courts to ensure speedy trials and strict punishment in paper leak cases. Announcing the move on X, he said, "Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. Have directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps in this regard.This continues our series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students. Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared."Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi rejected the Prime Minister's announcement, accusing the government of presiding over the collapse of the education system. In a post on X, he wrote: "You are the one who has harmed the future of our youth the most. You allowed and encouraged the total capture and destruction of our education system--and protected every person responsible for it." Rahul Gandhi also reiterated the Opposition's three demands — the immediate dismissal of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, an apology to students and action against those responsible for alleged police excesses during the protests.Responding to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's announcement, BJP President Nitin Nabin reiterated the government's commitment to accountability. He said, "The Prime Minister has stated before and continues to state that he is prepared to investigate every issue. He will introduce fast-track courts to ensure that those responsible face action. He will ensure that no culprit is spared. The nation and the world know that Narendra Modi's resolution means achieving it. "MPs from both the NDA and the INDIA bloc staged protests opposite each other at Makar Dwar within the Parliament premises in Delhi. The uproar continued inside the Lok Sabha, where proceedings were adjourned until 12 noon following loud protests by Opposition MPs.The Delhi high court will hear on Friday a public interest litigation (PIL) seeking a National Investigation Agency (NIA) probe into the July 20 "Sansad Chalo" protest over the alleged NEET paper leak.The matter was mentioned before a Bench comprising Chief Justice D.K. Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia. Appearing for the petitioner, Advocate Barun Kumar Sinha sought an urgent hearing, following which the court agreed to list the case for Friday.The PIL also seeks the transfer of all Delhi Police FIRs related to the protest to a specialised investigating agency and action against those allegedly involved in the violence.Sixteen Delhi Metro stations were closed from 7.30am on Thursday until further notice as security was tightened amid Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protests in the national capital. According to the Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC), entry and exit have been suspended at stations including Rajiv Chowk, Central Secretariat, Mandi House and Lok Kalyan Marg, although interchange facilities remain available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat."Service Update: The below-mentioned Metro stations will be closed from 07:30 AM till further instructions. However, interchange facilities shall remain available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House, and Central Secretariat," the DMRC said on X.Delhi Police on Thursday confirmed that police officers and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel were targeted during the Cockroach Janata Party's (CJP) 'Chalo Sansad' protest on July 20, after CCTV footage of the violence went viral on social media. Police said the video, which they confirmed was authentic, showed protesters pelting stones and vandalising property, including smashing glass windows at shops and a petrol pump. The clashes left more than 118 police personnel, including several senior officers and women officers, injured, while around 60 protesters were also hurt.The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) PM Modi's announcement on fast-track courts did not address accountability and reiterated its demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke shared an image of Pradhan captioned, "Hi, my name is nothing", while the party posted, "Dharmendra Pradhan Must Resign." CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka said, "The first thing is that it is good that after one-and-a-half months of protests, the prime minister has finally taken notice. Perhaps his foreign visits are over and he has returned to India, which is a good thing." He added, "But what punishment are you imposing after the paper leak has already taken place? That is not going to solve the problem of paper leaks. The real question is why paper leaks are happening in the first place. That question must be answered."On Wednesday night, fresh clashes broke out between protesters and security personnel at Jantar Mantar, with police firing tear gas shells and carrying out a baton charge to disperse the crowd. At least six police personnel, including two ACP-rank officers, were injured in separate incidents of alleged stone-pelting, according to police sources.Authorities said "anti-social" elements had infiltrated the protest, escalating tensions and attacking security personnel. Internet jammers were later activated in parts of central Delhi as security was tightened.
Article Summary: PM Modi said the Centre would establish fast-track courts to ensure speedy trials and strict punishment in paper leak cases. The announcement triggered sharp response from the opposition with Congress dismissing it as inadequate. Delhi high court will hear on Friday a public interest litigation (PIL) seeking a National Investigation Agency (NIA) probe into the July 20 "Sansad Chalo" protest.

Article Title: 'Assaulted protesters, grabbed Geetanjali Wangchuk's hair': CJP demands ADCP Sandeep Lamba's suspension after viral slap video
Article Text:
Opposition demanded strict action

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janata Party ( CJP ) on Thursday has demanded the suspension of Additional DCP Sandeep Lamba following the circulation of a viral video purportedly showing him slapping a woman protester during the recent student demonstrations at Jantar Mantar.In a post on X, the official CJP account demanded strict action against the officer. "First, he needs to be suspended pending investigation and then charged for illegally assaulting a young peaceful protester," it said.CJP also targeted Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, adding, "This is marginally more accountability than what Dharmendra Pradhan has faced even after 20 students died by suicide under his watch!"CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke earlier alleged that Lamba had also assaulted activist Sonam Wanghchuk’s wife Geetanjali during the protest."He is the same officer who climbed on the truck to hit Geetanjali Wangchuk before we all jumped in to save her. Later he grabbed her hair. Sandeep Lamba is not mentally fit to serve in the police," Dipke claimed.Congress general secretary KC Venugopal accused the Centre of sanctioning police excesses. "This is the true face of Amit Shah's goons in uniform," he wrote, alleging that police officers assaulted women students, removed their badges while beating protesters, and encouraged plain-clothed individuals to attack demonstrators."This level of brazenness cannot happen without sanction from the very top. Amit Shah cannot continue in his post. He has blood on his hands," Venugopal said.Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra also reacted strongly, saying the officer should face legal and social consequences. "No! He was not just doing his job! We need to name, shame and ensure social boycotts for each of these brutes and take them to court. First is Sandeep Lamba," she posted, urging legal teams to begin proceedings against the officer.Fresh violence erupted during the Cockroach Janta Party’s (CJP) protest in the national capital on Wednesday evening with large numbers of protesters gathered at the site. The area was also packed with police personnel deployed before the chaos erupted.
Article Summary: CJP demands suspension of Additional DCP Sandeep Lamba. Video purportedly shows Lamba slapping a woman protester. Opposition demands strict action against the officer. CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke earlier alleged that Lamba had also assaulted activist Sonam Wanghchuk’s wife Geetanjali.

Article Title: 'You harmed future of our youth most': Rahul Gandhi rebuts PM's fast-track courts announcement, repeats 3 demands
Article Text:
The leader of opposition in Lok Sabha also reiterated the demands raised by protesting students

Students have lost faith in the system: Priyanka Gandhi

Poll Vote & Share your view What is the most important step the government should take to restore students' faith in the education system? Implement stricter regulations Improve accountability and oversight 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi on Thursday hit back at Prime Minister Narendra Modi over his announcement that fast-track courts would be set up to deal with paper leak cases.In a post on X, Rahul Gandhi rejected PM Modi's assertion that the government was protecting students' interests. He wrote, "You are the one who has harmed the future of our youth the most. "He further alleged that the government had allowed the country's education system to deteriorate. "You allowed and encouraged the total capture and destruction of our education system - and protected every person responsible for it," Rahul Gandhi said.The leader of opposition in Lok Sabha also reiterated the demands raised by protesting students: "1. Sack Dharmendra Pradhan. 2. Apologise to the students. 3. Take action against those who assaulted them."Rahul Gandhi's remarks came shortly after PM Modi announced that fast-track courts would be established to ensure swift punishment for those involved in paper leaks.This came after PM Modi in a post on X wrote :"Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks."He added that the authorities had been directed to take all necessary steps. "This continues our series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students. Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," PM Modi wrote.Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to respond directly to protesting students after his announcements of fast-track courts for paper leak cases. She said that the government must address the students' core demands rather than rely solely on institutional measures, saying public trust in the system had eroded following recent events."He is a leader, and the time has come to show leadership. Listen to the demands of the students; they are demanding the resignation of the minister and action against those who have beaten them up. The students have lost faith in the system. If you provide a solution within the framework of the system, the students will not accept it. A solution can be reached only when you agree to the demands of the students. You tweet that you have faith in the students, but last night you were beating them up. If you have faith in the students, then why are the students being lathi-charged? If you are worried about the students, then call and speak with them," she said.Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh also criticised the Prime Minister over the announcement. He said PM Modi was avoiding a discussion in Parliament on the issue."The PM does not have the courage to stand up in Parliament, speak and engage in serious debate like all his predecessors did when the occasion demanded," Ramesh wrote on X.He claimed the Prime Minister's social media post reflected pressure from the ongoing protests. "Actually, the post such as it is demonstrates that he is shaken and rattled," Ramesh said. He also targeted the government over alleged irregularities, writing, "Vote Chori, Seat Chori, Chanda Chori, Exam Paper Chori…. the list goes on and on."Protests over the NEET paper leak controversy continued in Delhi and several other cities. The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party has been demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation, action against those responsible for the paper leak, and accountability from the Centre.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi rejects PM Modi's assertion that the government was protecting students' interests. The leader of opposition in Lok Sabha also reiterated the demands raised by protesting students. PM Modi announced that fast-track courts would be established to ensure swift punishment for those involved in paper leaks.

Article Title: 'Nothing more important than youth's future': PM Modi announces fast-track courts for paper leak cases
Article Text:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday announced that the Centre will set up fast-track courts to ensure the speedy trial and stringent punishment of those involved in paper leak cases, asserting that protecting students' future remains the government's top priority.In a post on X, the Prime Minister said, "Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. Have directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps in this regard. This continues our series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students."He added: "Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared."The announcement comes amid a prolonged student protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar over NEET paper leak fiasco.However, Rahul Gandhi was quick to hit back at Prime Minister Modi's announcement, holding him directly responsible for the crisis and saying, "You are the one who has harmed the future of our youth the most."Stepping up his attack, Lok Sabha leader of opposition alleged, "You allowed and encouraged the total capture and destruction of our education system - and protected every person responsible for it."He also reiterated the students' demands, listing three key demands: "1. Sack Dharmendra Pradhan. 2. Apologise to the students. 3. Take action against those who assaulted them."Earlier this week, Rahul led a march towards the Prime Minister's residence, demanding accountability and seeking the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union home minister Amit Shah, and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Samajwadi Party supremo Akhilesh Yadav and Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi joined the Lok Sabha leader of opposition during the protest.The march, however, ended in a scuffle with security personnel, following which the opposition leaders were briefly detained.Meanwhile, the Centre's announcement comes days after Prime Minister Modi called the paper leak menace as a "ghor paap" (grave sin) and vowed strict action against those who "play with the future of youth."The remarks were made during the NDA parliamentary party meeting and were later conveyed by parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju.According to Rijiju, the Prime Minister said the "NEET paper leak should not be an issue of partisan politics" as it is a "national issue and not a matter of one state or the Centre alone."
Article Summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the Centre will set up fast-track courts to ensure speedy trial and stringent punishment of those involved in paper leak cases. The announcement comes amid a prolonged student protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar over NEET paper leak fiasco. Rahul Gandhi was quick to hit back at Prime Minister Modi's announcement, holding him directly responsible for the crisis.

Article Title: 'Nature's call' pretext for detour: Office cab driver confesses to raping Hyderabad woman on way to airport
Article Text:
Image Used For Representational Purpose Only

Poll Vote & Share your view What should be the top priority to improve passenger safety in cab services? Stricter driver verification Better safety features and monitoring 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

HYDERABAD: A 37-year-old cabin crew member of a private airline was allegedly raped by her cab driver while travelling to Rajiv Gandhi International (RGI) Airport, Shamshabad, to report for duty on Monday.Based on her complaint, the RGIA outpost police registered a rape case and arrested the accused on Tuesday. He was later remanded in judicial custody.According to police, the incident occurred around 2 pm when the woman was travelling from her residence to the airport in an office cab.Using details provided by her, police identified the accused as a 23-year-old driver from Siddhanti area in Shamshabad and apprehended him shortly thereafter. “We arrested the accused from Shamshabad. During the interrogation, he confessed to committing the offence,” RGIA outpost inspector B Kiran Kumar said.As the vehicle approached the airport, the driver allegedly took a detour through a low traffic stretch, claiming he needed to attend nature’s call.“He was not supposed to take a detour, but since he told the woman that he had to attend a nature’s call, she did not object.He stopped the car near an under-construction international school and got out. A little while later, he suddenly opened the back door and forced himself upon the woman inside the cab,” said an RGIA police official.Police said the accused fled the spot in the cab after the incident, leaving the woman behind, as she raised an alarm. With the help of a worker, she contacted police, who shifted her to Bharosa Centre for counselling.Based on her statement, the RGIA outpost police registered a case under section 64 (rape) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. The woman was sent for medical examination on the same day.
Article Summary: A 37-year-old cabin crew member of a private airline was allegedly raped by her cab driver. She was travelling to Rajiv Gandhi International (RGI) Airport, Shamshabad, to report for duty. The driver allegedly took a detour through a low traffic stretch, claiming he needed to attend nature’s call.

Article Title: 'Who will be responsible if we lose our jobs?': Commuters stranded as 16 Delhi metro stations shut amid CJP protest
Article Text:
A video shared by news agency PTI showed commuters waiting inside Mandi House Metro station as security personnel restricted exits.

NEW DELHI: Commuters were left stranded and frustrated on Wednesday after 16 Delhi metro stations were closed due to security reasons amid the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) protest in central Delhi, with several passengers questioning how they would reach work on time.A video shared by news agency PTI showed commuters waiting inside Mandi House Metro station as security personnel restricted exits. Many were seen arguing with DMRC officials and police personnel, seeking clarity on when they would be allowed to leave."Who will be responsible if we lose our jobs?" one commuter asked in the video.Others were heard saying, "We have been stuck here for more than half an hour", "What is the sense of all this?", "Tell us who should we talk to", and "Let us talk to the responsible officer." One commuter also said, "Mera to office bhi yahi hai (My office is nearby)."The video also captured people saying that slogans were being raised at the station while officials repeatedly indicated that they had no further information about when normal movement would resume.Earlier in the day, the Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) announced that 16 stations had been closed till further notice due to security reasons.The affected stations are Lok Kalyan Marg, Rajiv Chowk, Patel Chowk, Ramakrishna Ashram Marg, Barakhambha Road, Supreme Court, Seva Teerth, Janpath, Mandi House, Central Secretariat, ITO, Delhi Gate, Indraprastha, Khan Market, Jor Bagh and Shivaji Stadium.DMRC said interchange facilities would remain available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat despite the closures.The closures came amid heightened security in central Delhi as the CJP's sit-in protest at Jantar Mantar entered its third day following Monday's 'Sansad Chalo' march, which witnessed clashes between protesters and police near Parliament.The party has said it will continue its agitation until Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.
Article Summary: 16 Delhi metro stations closed due to security reasons amid Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) protest in central Delhi. Video shows commuters waiting inside Mandi House Metro station as security personnel restricted exits. Many were seen arguing with DMRC officials and police personnel, seeking clarity on when they would be allowed to leave.

Article Title: 'Does India run on your arrogance?' BJP tears into Rahul Gandhi over PM residence march
Article Text:
BJP calls Rahul's march to PM residence 'irresponsible conduct'

Poll Vote & Share your view What should be the top priority to prevent paper leaks in competitive exams? Enhanced security measures Strict action against offenders Better transparency in exam processes 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: A day after Rahul Gandhi's march to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence ended in a confrontation with police, the BJP mounted a sharp political counteroffensive on Wednesday, accusing Lok Sabha leader of opposition of disregarding the dignity of the Prime Minister's residence and announcing nationwide protests against Congress party.The saffron party also called out Rahul Gandhi's "Chalo PM Residence" as "irresponsible" and "highly condemnable." Leading the attack, senior party leader Ravi Shankar Prasad asked Lok Sabha LoP: "'Does this country run on your arrogance?"Addressing a presser in New Delhi, senior BJP leader and former Union minister Ravi Shankar Prasad took a swipe at Rahul Gandhi's political legacy, saying that someone from a family that produced three Prime Ministers should have understood the dignity attached to the Prime Minister's residence."He is sitting on a dharna at the gate of the Prime Minister's residence. His father Rajiv Gandhi was the Prime Minister, his grandmother Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister, and his great-grandfather Jawaharlal Nehru was also the Prime Minister of India. He, of all people, should know the dignity and sanctity of the Prime Minister's residence. Despite this, the kind of irresponsible behaviour he has displayed is highly condemnable," Prasad said.Announcing the BJP's response, Prasad said the saffron party would stage protests across the country outside Congress offices."The BJP will hold protests today across the country against Congress outside Congress offices for the unruly protest outside the PM's house yesterday," he said.Questioning Rahul Gandhi's approach, Prasad accused the Congress leader of abandoning dialogue and trying to dictate terms to the government."How do you intend to function? Will you just sit there forcibly? You were engaged in a dialogue. They were ready for a debate on the NEET issue, but then you backed out. Does this country run on your stick? Will it run on your arrogance? Rahul Gandhi, you have been irresponsible. I condemn your entire conduct," he said.Defending the police action during the protest, the BJP leader said security personnel had no option but to intervene to maintain law and order in the high-security zone."Later, the police were forced to take action, which was necessary for security and for maintaining peace. The Prime Minister has always been clear on these issues," Prasad said.Shifting focus to the controversy surrounding competitive examinations, Prasad asserted that the government had acted swiftly to protect the integrity of recruitment and entrance tests."In the past, irregularities were reported in several states during various examinations. Consequently, foolproof exams were conducted immediately after cancellations. The results have been declared with a 56-57% success rate, and students are satisfied. These exams were held without charging any additional fees," he told reporters.Emphasising the Centre's commitment to students, Prasad said those responsible for exam irregularities had been arrested and were facing trial."All the candidates appearing for NEET and other exams are our children. It is our duty to be concerned about their future, and we will fulfill that responsibility. Those involved in irregularities have been arrested and are facing trial. The government is ensuring that their trial leads to certain punishment," he said.The senior BJP leader concluded by reiterating Prime Minister Narendra Modi's assurance that the sanctity of NEET and other examinations would be protected through systemic reforms and stricter scrutiny.Meanwhile, another voice from the ruling BJP joined the attack on Rahul Gandhi. Senior BJP leader and MP Giriraj Singh told reporters, "Rahul Gandhi has tarnished the dignity of the Constitution while holding a constitutional position. His conduct is extremely dangerous. A case of sedition should be filed against Rahul Gandhi."The BJP's attack came a day after Rahul led the Opposition's march towards Lok Kalyan Marg, the official residence of the Prime Minister, where protesters came face-to-face with security personnel in the high-security Lutyens' Delhi zone.Videos from the scene showed police moving in to disperse the protesters gathered outside the Prime Minister's residence. In one video shared by the Congress on its official X handle, Rahul Gandhi was seen lying on the road as police personnel attempted to lift and remove him from the protest site. The former Congress president appeared to resist being taken away as security officials surrounded him.The Congress also released a photograph showing Rahul nosebleed following the police action, alleging excessive use of force during the protest.Rahul Gandhi's protest came amid Jantar Mantar protest demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over accountability in the NEET paper-leak.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi's march to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence ended in confrontation with police. Opposition leader accused of disregarding the dignity of the Prime Minister's residence. Senior BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad said Rahul Gandhi's behaviour was "irresponsible" and "highly condemnable" He said the saffron party would stage protests outside Congress offices.

Article Title: 'Bail by HCs shouldn't be challenged in SC': CJI-led bench rejects appeal by Chhattisgarh government
Article Text:
SC said that once a high court grants bail to an accused, it must be treated as final

NEW DELHI: In an important exposition on the working of criminal justice process without diluting the primacy of individual liberty, the Supreme Court on Wednesday said that once a high court grants bail to an accused, it must be treated as final and SC must not entertain appeals against the order by the state, reports DhananjayMahapatra.A bench of CJI Surya Kant, and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana made the statement as they disposed of Chhattisgarh govt's appeal against grant of bail to Chaitanya Baghel, son of ex-CM Bhupesh Baghel, despite having serious reservations about HC's order, and said judicial system must get back to the trend during 1980s, when then CJI P N Bhagwati had said HC's orders granting bail should be final.CJI Surya Kant said HCs appear to have become very conservative in granting bail and write 40-50 pages while granting or rejecting bail without realising the impact of long discussions on merits of the case in a fair and impartial trial. The bail orders should not exceed three to four pages.Justice Bagchi suggested that appeals to SC in matters of bail should be made in cases where it has been denied. "Cases where liberty is deprived stand on a completely different footing in SC which is the enforcer of fundamental rights. When it is cancellation of bail, that is diminution of liberty, we must weigh it against victim's rights and the societal interest.These are the guiding principles."The bench said the states' penchant for challenging bails granted by HCs in the last two decades is the single largest reason for the burgeoning of appeals in SC. "Every day each bench of SC deals with at least ten such petitions. This is the concern of the court," he said."Why we are concerned -if a prosecutor and investigator rests so much on interlocutory relief, the ultimate effort for securing conviction is lessened... by seeking cancellation of bail you (state) can ensure that an undertrial prisoner continues to be incarcerated while absolving yourself of the higher responsibility of ensuring fair and just trial to secure conviction, which the victims of crime desire," the bench said."In most cases there is no honourable acquittal. The accused is not convicted because the investigator slips and the prosecution fails to present evidence properly. This is where the state must fasten accountability and not seek cancellation of bail. We are making these observations out of serious concern," it further said.Justice Bagchi said, "The HCs are passing 50-page bail orders in UAPA cases, 40-page orders in PMLA cases. Why? Do the judges understand the consequences of such lengthy bail orders?" When Kapil Sibal attempted to argue in favour of upholding Baghel's bail, the bench said, "Please do not bring up the facts of your case. There are some very serious reservations about your case."The bench said even in MISA (Maintenance of Internal Security Act enforced during the Emergency) detentions, HCs used to release detenues by passing one-page orders - "we are not satisfied with grounds of arrest, as communicated, and hence detention order is set aside".Justice Bagchi said CJI Kant-authored judgment in K A Najeeb case in 2021 had laid down guidelines for grant of bail and cleared doubts in minds of HC judges who were finding it difficult to strike a balance between liberty and continued detention. In Najeeb judgment, SC had said courts can grant bail to the accused on the ground of long incarceration even in UAPA cases.Solicitor general Tushar Mehta had argued Baghel's case was no ordinary one. "This case involves a peculiarity. A large number of WhatsApp texts... were exchanged between two constitutional authorities - the judge and then Chhattisgarh advocate general."
Article Summary: Supreme Court said that once a high court grants bail to an accused, it must be treated as final and SC must not entertain appeals against the order by the state. A bench of CJI Surya Kant, and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana made the statement as they disposed of Chhattisgarh govt's appeal against grant of bail.

Article Title: Complaint filed in NHRC over police ‘brutality’ on protesters at Jantar Mantar
Article Text:
Complaint filed with NHRC seeking independent inquiry into the alleged police “brutality” on July 20

NEW DELHI: A complaint has been filed with National Human Rights Commission (NHRC), seeking independent inquiry into the alleged police “brutality” on July 20 against students protesting over the NEET-UG exam paper leak issue here, including allegations of misconduct by male police personnel against female students.Congress member and advocate Virender Vashist in the complaint also urged the commission to look into allegations that protesting students, who were exercising their fundamental rights guaranteed by the Constitution, were subjected to “excessive and disproportionate use of force by police”.“The incident has caused widespread concern among students, parents, and members of civil society. The allegations of excessive force and misconduct by law enforcement authorities demand an independent, impartial, and transparent inquiry to uphold the rule of law,” the complaint stated.He has urged the panel to direct its investigation division to conduct an impartial inquiry into the incident; constitute an NHRC team for a visit to Jantar Mantar and interact with victim students, eyewitnesses; record statements of affected female students; call for detailed reports, CCTV footage, body-camera recordings, deployment records, medical records, and all other relevant material from Delhi Police and other authorities concerned.The complainant has urged the NHRC to recommend appropriate action against any officials found responsible for the violations and also recommend compensation and any other appropriate relief to the victims.
Article Summary: Complaint filed with NHRC seeking independent inquiry into the alleged police ‘brutality’ on July 20 against students protesting over the NEET-UG exam paper leak issue. Congress member and advocate Virender Vashist in the complaint also urged the commission to look into allegations that protesting students were subjected to “excessive and disproportionate use of force by police”

Article Title: Politics of silence: Is NCP(SP) chief Sharad Pawar keeping options open on Delimitation Bill?
Article Text:
Pressure to keep his flock together

The balancing act

On July 7, Maharashtra chief minister and senior BJP leader Devendra Fadnavis announced that no new party will be joining the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) or the state's Mahayuti alliance at present.

On July 8, Sharad Pawar paid a courtesy visit to Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde at the Vidhan Bhavan complex. Pawar also met his party MLAs at Shinde’s office.

On July 12, NCP (SP) leader Eknath Khadse, currently a member of the legislative council (MLC), acknowledged that he had travelled to Delhi and had met BJP general secretary Vinod Tawde. Khadse claimed that Amit Shah had called him and asked him to join the BJP.

On July 15, NCP(SP) leader Jayant Patil met Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis to discuss the disqualification of the mayor of Urun-Ishwarpur in his constituency. Interestingly on the same night ruling NCP leaders Praful Patel and Sunil Tatkare also met Fadnavis.

When Sharad Pawar differed with allies

'If you are being discussed ... '

NEW DELHI: Will Sharad Pawar ’s NCP(SP) support BJP’s Delimitation Bill if the NDA government decides to bring it again in the monsoon session of Parliament? While speculations do the rounds, the Maratha strongman remains conspicuously silent and lets his actions keep everyone guessing. So, while on Tuesday Sharad Pawar joined forces with the opposition at Jantar Mantar where students are protesting against NEET paper leak and seeking education minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation, on Wednesday he was meeting Prime Minister Narendra Modi along with his daughter MP Supriya Sule .In June, there were several reports to suggest that Sharad Pawar’s NCP(SP) could be considering a merger with the Congress to prevent any possible poaching of his legislators. A month later in July, there are strong speculations that Sharad Pawar is leaning towards the NDA to secure the future of his party in the twilight years of his politics.Does this come as a surprise? Well, definitely not. When it comes to politics of ambiguity, no one can beat Sharad Pawar - who is known to keep his cards close to his chest. And that perhaps explains why there is suspense around the line his party could possibly take on the Delimitation Bill.His daughter Supriya Sule, who is MP from Baramati, has rejected all speculations on backing or joining the NDA and has maintained that the party is yet to take any stand on the Delimitation Bill.But her remark last week: “if the proposed Delimitation Bill- a key legislative agenda of the NDA government- is based on a uniform 50 per cent increase in seats across all states, there would be little reason to oppose it," has already set tongues wagging.Sharad Pawar has at present 8 members in Lok Sabha and 10 MLAs in Maharashtra assembly. With the NDA government working aggressively behind the scenes to reach the magic figure of two-thirds majority in Lok Sabha for the Delimitation Bill, Sharad Pawar knows that his MPs are vulnerable. He has just seen Uddhav Thackeray helplessly lose his 6 MPs to Eknath Shinde ’s Shiv Sena. Both Uddhav Thackeray and Sharad Pawar have suffered coups that split their parties vertically. While Shinde revolted against Uddhav and took control of the Shiv Sena, in NCP the revolt was led by the late Ajit Pawar, who rebelled against his uncle Sharad in 2023 and wrested control of the party.After a dismal show in the 2024 assembly elections, Pawar knows that his politics and influence is on the wane and his legislators could be lured with promises of greener pastures. Seen in this context, Sharad Pawar’s silence and his mixed signals could be a weapon to keep his legislators hopeful and guessing about his next moves.Those who have followed Sharad Pawar’s politics, will recount many such instances when he used his power of silence to convey mixed signals to opposing camps. One such example was in 2019 when Sharad Pawar was trying to stitch anti-BJP front in Maharashtra with Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena and the Congress, while on the other hand, at the same time, his nephew Ajit Pawar was negotiating with the BJP. The two-track politics eventually resulted in a pre-dawn swearing-in of Devendra Fadnavis as chief minister and Ajit Pawar as deputy chief minister. However, the government was short-lived as Sharad Pawar was forced to dump Ajit Pawar then. But questions were raised even then if Sharad knew about Ajit’s moves and had allowed both the channels to progress simultaneously. Years later, Sharad Pawar dropped enough hints to suggest that he actually knew about the Track 2 manoeuvres by his nephew then.So while Sharad Pawar remains silent, let’s analyze some of the developments in the recent past to understand his politics of ambiguity.These developments triggered speculations around NCP(SP)'s possible support to Delimitation Bill or the possible merger of the two NCP factions (one led by Sharad Pawar and the other led by Sunetra Pawar, wife of late Ajit Pawar) at the behest of the BJP. As expected, Sule denied both. "Neither the BJP nor the Congress nor any NDA ally has contacted us. When there is no proposal, the question of merger or joining any alliance does not arise," the Baramati MP said. She also denied any political planning behind these developments and labelled them as “courtesy visit”, “unexpected meeting” and “coincidence”. Sule claimed that the NCP(SP) chief’s meeting with Shinde was "purely a coincidence". On NCP (SP) leader Jayant Patil's meeting with Fadnavis, she said Patil had officially sought an appointment to raise the issue of action taken against a municipal council president belonging to the Sharad Pawar-led party.Sule has also dismissed reports that party MPs and MLAs were under pressure to join the ruling alliance and claimed that all eight NCP (SP) MPs firmly rally behind party chief Sharad Pawar. "We meet regularly. None of our MPs or MLAs has told me that they want to take a different political stand. Everyone is happy where they are," she said.Sharad Pawar is known for breaking ranks with allies and taking independent stand. When all regional parties were determined to isolate Congress and make a third front excluding both BJP and Congress, it was Sharad Pawar who said that any opposition alliance without Congress would not be effective. Similarly, it was Sharad Pawar who once advocated that regional parties that were born out of Congress should consider “ghar wapsi”.Just as Pawar broke ranks to back Congress on some occasions, he did the same to back Prime Minister Narendra Modi and the NDA government on other occasions. Often, Pawar’s praise for PM Modi and his policies have generated political buzz. While all opposition parties, including the Congress, were targeting PM Modi over his many foreign trips, Sharad Pawar had a different take. In May this year he praised the Prime Minister for working to uphold India's prestige on the global stage. "Political differences should not come in the way when it comes to protecting India's prestige, and PM Modi is working to uphold the country's honour abroad," Pawar stated.While it may not be easy to comprehend what guides Sharad Pawar’s politics of silence, this one remark from his daughter Supriya Sule perhaps gives some insight into his thought processes. Responding to constant media scrutiny, the Baramati MP recently invoked a lesson from her father and said: "Sharad Pawar always says that if you are being discussed -- whether for good or bad reasons -- it means your political currency is still strong and sound. "That explains why it has always been difficult to predict the politics of Sharad Pawar. His politics of silence and his art of conveying mixed signals to friends and foes are perhaps his weapons to keep all possibilities open - for we all know there are no permanent friends or permanent enemies in politics.
Article Summary: Sharad Pawar has at present 8 members in Lok Sabha and 10 MLAs in Maharashtra assembly. He has just seen Uddhav Thackeray helplessly lose his 6 MPs to Eknath Shinde ’s Shiv Sena. His daughter Supriya Sule has rejected all speculations on backing or joining the NDA.

Article Title: Sharad Pawar meets PM Modi, timing fuels buzz of delimit bill talk
Article Text:
PM Modi meets NCP (SP) President Sharad Pawar, at Parliament House, in New Delhi.

NEW DELHI: A day after reaching Jantar Mantar to express solidarity with CJP protesters, NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar and his daughter Supriya Sule met PM Narendra Modi on Wednesday.NCP (SP) sources said the meeting focused on Maharashtra's agrarian crisis, with Pawar raising demands including a legal guarantee for minimum support price (MSP), while Sule invited Modi to her daughter's wedding reception in Delhi.The timing of the meeting drew attention as Pawar's NCP (SP), which has eight Lok Sabha MPs, is seen to be warming up to BJP's outreach to key opposition parties to bolster support for the Constitution amendment bill on women's reservation and the delimitation bill.Pictures shared by the Prime Minister's Office showed Modi warmly leading Pawar by the hand into his office.The two bills, which seek to implement women's reservation in legislatures from 2029, were defeated in the last session. However, BJP's support base has since expanded following the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led faction and the split in the Trinamool Congress, with 20 MPs declaring support for the NDA. If the eight MPs of NCP (Sharad Pawar) also back the bills, BJP would come within striking distance of the two-thirds majority required for their passage.NCP (SP) state chief Shashikant Shinde denied any political motive, saying Pawar had only conveyed the concerns of the protesting students and urged the Centre to hold talks with them. "Our stand on the delimitation bill is already clear," he said.However, many remained unconvinced, given Pawar's reputation for keeping his cards close to his chest.
Article Summary: Sharad Pawar and his daughter Supriya Sule met PM Narendra Modi on Wednesday. Meeting focused on Maharashtra's agrarian crisis, sources said. Sule invited Modi to her daughter's wedding reception in Delhi. Meeting drew attention as Pawar's NCP (SP) is seen to be warming up to BJP's outreach.

Article Title: Quad seeks free & open Indo-Pacific, pushes back against Chinese assertiveness
Article Text:
Jaishankar with Australian foreign minister Penny Wong, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Japan foreign minister Toshimitsu Motegi during Quad Foreign Ministers Meeting

India's policy of "multi-alignment" and strategic autonomy was evident in Manila Wednesday as external affairs minister S Jaishankar met his China counterpart Wang Yi in the Philippines and also joined a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers that had US secretary of state Marco Rubio declaring Quad remained a priority and will meet again later this year.The Quad meeting saw discussions on the situation in East China Sea and South China Sea, with Japan opposing any unilateral attempt to change the status quo by force or coercion and expressing grave concerns over export restrictions, including on critical minerals. The Quad meeting took place amid escalating China-Philippines tensions over another flare-up in the South China Sea.Even in the absence of a summit, Quad comprising Australia, India, Japan and the US has remained functional with the foreign ministers meeting for the second time this year, after they met in Delhi in May. Jaishankar, Rubio and their Australia and Japanese counterparts, Penny Wong and Toshimitsu Motegi respectively, met on the margins of the ASEAN-related meetings in Manila.In a joint press release, they reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening a free and open Indo-Pacific and their “unwavering” support for ASEAN unity and centrality, while also declaring they were united in their conviction that peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific maritime domain underpin the security and prosperity of the region.“We are deeply invested in the region’s success and in cooperating with ASEAN and its member states,” said the release. The statement outlined shared priorities related to maritime security and economic security, critical and emerging technologies, and humanitarian assistance in support for the implementation of the ASEAN Outlook on the Indo-Pacific (AOIP), which envisages ASEAN Centrality as the underlying principle for promoting cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.According to a Japanese readout, the ministers looked forward to the convening of the next Quad Leaders’ Summit. However, there was no word on the dates for the summit that India has tried unsuccessfully to host so far. In fact, unlike the joint statement issued after the meeting in Delhi, the joint press release made no mention of the summit. With President Donald Trump unlikely to visit India this year, the summit now is expected to take place only on the margins of the G20 summit in the US in December. The foreign ministers are likely to meet again though on the sidelines of a G20 ministerial in Atlanta in October.“The United States, India, Australia, and Japan share a vision of a free and open Indo-Pacific that is grounded in the rule of law, sovereignty, and territorial integrity,” said Rubio in a post on X, adding that strong cooperation is vital to supporting ASEAN’s own priority and centrality in the region.US ambassador Sergio Gor, who joined Rubio for the Quad meeting, said tere was a very productive discussion on delivering results related to a free and open Indo-Pacific. “Our four nations share a common vision,” he said.
Article Summary: India's policy of "multi-alignment" and strategic autonomy was evident in Manila Wednesday. External affairs minister S Jaishankar met his China counterpart Wang Yi in the Philippines. He also joined a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers that had US secretary of state Marco Rubio declaring Quad remained a priority.

Article Title: SC acquits blast accused in jail for 23 years
Article Text:
Apex court acquits 1996 blast accused after he spends 23 years in prison

NEW DELHI: Supreme Court Wednesday acquitted a man accused in the 1996 Rajasthan bomb blast case after finding no evidence against him, though he had already spent 23 years in jail. The court found the prosecution case against him "to be woefully wanting and lacking in merit" and directed his release.A bench of Justices Vikram Nath, Sanjay Karol and Sandeep Mehta said there was no evidence against him, and the trial court and Rajasthan high court wrongly convicted him on the basis of his confessional statements, "which themselves suffer from serious infirmities", and the police failed to place any independent and reliable material against him.In this case, only two accused were convicted - the main accused, Dr Abdul Hameed, was awarded a death sentence, and this man, Pappu, alias Salim, was given life imprisonment.As Hameed was not given proper legal aid to defend himself, SC quashed his conviction and ordered a fresh trial against him but acquitted Salim on merit for lack of evidence.Hameed has remained in jail for the last 29 years. Salim, however, was released on parole during pendency of the case when he completed 23 years in jail.
Article Summary: Supreme Court acquits man accused in 1996 Rajasthan bomb blast case. Court finds no evidence against him, though he had already spent 23 years in jail. The court found the prosecution case against him "to be woefully wanting and lacking in merit"

Article Title: Centre unlikely to sack Pradhan over NEET row, open to compensate victims' kin: Report
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Centre is unlikely to remove Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan despite mounting demands for his resignation, as it believes he cannot be held responsible for the NEET paper leak, news agency PTI reported, citing a senior government functionary.According to the report, the government is, however, willing to favourably consider the demand for compensation for the families of students who died by suicide following the alleged paper leak.The Centre is also open to engaging seriously with protesters led by the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP), though it believes there should be flexibility over the venue of the talks, instead of insisting on a particular location, the functionary told PTI.The report added that the government has held a series of meetings on the issue involving Union home minister Amit Shah, health minister JP Nadda, commerce minister Piyush Goyal and Pradhan.The development comes after Nadda, at a press conference on Wednesday, reiterated the Centre's willingness to discuss the alleged NEET paper leak in Parliament under any format and for any duration agreed upon by the Opposition."We have wanted this issue to be discussed in Parliament. Whether it is a short-duration discussion or a long-duration discussion, you decide how much time you want. The government is ready for discussion, and the Opposition should not keep changing the goalpost," he said.Calling Parliament the appropriate forum to address the issue, Nadda urged both the government and the opposition to work together on a long-term solution."Children are ours, yours, and the future of the country. It is the responsibility of both the government and the Opposition to protect their future. We should discuss every aspect and come up with a concrete policy," he said.Earlier in the day, leader of opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi called for Pradhan's resignation, saying it was among the protesters' "non-negotiable" demands.The CJP has been spearheading protests over the alleged examination irregularities since June.Its Parliament march on July 20 saw students and supporters attempting to reach Parliament despite heavy police barricading, lathi-charge and the use of tear gas. The march was eventually halted near Parliament Street, where multiple layers of barricades had been erected.
Article Summary: The Centre is unlikely to remove education minister Dharmendra Pradhan despite mounting demands for his resignation. The government is, however, willing to consider the demand for compensation for the families of students who died by suicide following the alleged paper leak. The Centre is also open to engaging seriously with protesters led by the Cockroach Janata Party.

Article Title: Terrorist kill J&K cop in Anantnag market, first strike since Pahalgam attack
Article Text:
Police officials carry the mortal remains of the Police constable who was killed in a terrorist attack

SRINAGAR: A terrorist shot dead a J&K police head constable in Lal Chowk market of south Kashmir’s Anantnag town when it was bustling with shoppers around 12.30pm Wednesday. This marks the first attack in Kashmir since the Pahalgam massacre that left 26 people dead on April 22, 2025.Ashiq Hussain Qureshi, attached to IRP 3rd battalion, was part of a team deployed to secure the “Lal Chowk-Kokernag axis”. He suffered critical bullet wounds and later died, Kashmir IGP VK Birdi said. Qureshi’s team retaliated but the terrorist escaped by blending into the crowd.According to a shopkeeper, two bullets struck his shop. “We heard gunshots and ducked down inside the shop. Later we saw a policeman lying on the road,” he said.Security forces sealed the area and launched a search. Police also cracked down on suspected terrorist networks, detaining many people for questioning. United Liberation Front J&K, a shadow group linked to The Resistance Front, which is an offshoot of Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba, claimed responsibility for the attack.A CCTV footage purportedly showing the attack spread online, prompting police to urge people not to circulate the video and warned of strict legal action against violators.The brazen attack in a busy market came during Amarnath Yatra, which began July 3 but has remained suspended since July 19 because of heavy rain and landslides. The drive from Lal Chowk to Nunwan base camp in Pahalgam, a starting point for the annual pilgrimage, takes just over an hour.Security was further tightened along Jammu-Srinagar NH-44 and across Kashmir valley. Lal Chowk is about 3km from the NH-44 intersection at Khanabal.Qureshi from Lalpora village in central Kashmir’s Budgam is survived by his wife, two sons, a daughter, and his elderly mother. His father, also a policeman, died in the line of duty in an accident at Banihal 15 years ago. Mourners streamed into the family’s home as grief swept through the village.Lieutenant governor Manoj Sinha and CM Omar Abdullah condemned the attack. “My heartfelt condolences to the family of head constable Ashiq Hussain Qureshi, who sacrificed his life in the line of duty… This heinous act will not go unpunished,” Sinha said.BJP called the attack a desperate attempt by anti-national elements to disrupt Kashmir’s peaceful atmosphere and derail Amarnath Yatra, which it said had drawn an unprecedented response from pilgrims.Senior police officers described the killing as a rare strike, saying attacks targeting security forces had virtually disappeared from Kashmir valley over the past several years.The previous attack targeting a police officer took place on Oct 29, 2023, when terrorists shot inspector Masroor Ahmad Wani in Srinagar’s Eidgah grounds when he was off duty and playing cricket with friends. He died at AIIMS New Delhi on Dec 7.Lal Chowk in Anantnag last saw a major terrorist strike on Aug 9, 2021, when BJP functionary and sarpanch Ghulam Rasool Dar and his wife Jawahira Begum were shot dead in their home. In June 2019, terrorists attacked a CRPF team on KP Road near Anantnag, killing five personnel. Inspector Arshad Khan, who engaged the attackers at close range, later died of his wounds and was posthumously awarded the Shaurya Chakra.
Article Summary: Ashiq Hussain Qureshi, attached to IRP 3rd battalion, was part of a team deployed to secure the “Lal Chowk-Kokernag axis’ He suffered critical bullet wounds and later died. United Liberation Front J&K, a shadow group linked to The Resistance Front, claimed responsibility for the attack.

Article Title: 'Arrogance not good': CJP, Opposition react to Supreme Court saying they don't have time to watch protest videos
Article Text:
Opposi​tion, CJP react TO CJI remark (PTI, ANI images)​

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) and some opposition leaders on Wednesday criticised Chief Justice of India Surya Kant's remarks after the Supreme Court declined urgent listing of a plea on police action during the July 20 student protest in Delhi.The criticism followed CJI Surya Kant's remarks where he said, "Don't waste our time," while declining an urgent hearing on the plea. When the petitioner's counsel referred to videos allegedly showing police atrocities on students, the CJI responded, "We are not interested in videos, we don't have time to watch."Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke reacted with "????????????" on X to the CJI judgement, while its spokesperson Saurav Das criticised the court's remarks, saying, "Our Supreme Court deserves better. Surya Kant ji must realise that so much arrogance is not good. Government of India must counsel him."Reacting to the observations, Aam Aadmi Party leader Somnath Bharti said the issue raised larger concerns about access to justice."Its a dangerous situation. If Honble CJI wont listen to such issues then who will listen? And this brings a larger question before the people of India, does a court have a choice to listen or not to listen issues against the people in power?? They may decide this way or that way but in my humble considered opinion, high court or supreme court is duty bound to give patient hearing to issues, as important as brutalities committed on Students yesterday,"he wrote on X.Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Priyanka Chaturvedi reacted sarcastically with a one-word post: "Amazing."Thousands of students and young protesters had marched towards Parliament on July 20 demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations.The protest turned violent after demonstrators attempted to breach police barricades. Security personnel used lathis and tear gas to disperse the crowd, with both protesters and police reporting injuries.Meanwhile, the CJP's sit-in at Jantar Mantar entered its second month on Wednesday, with the organisation claiming that more supporters joined the protest overnight.These development come months after CJI Surya Kant, while hearing a separate case, had said, "There are youngsters like cockroaches, who don't get any employment and don't have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, some of them become RTI activists, some of them become other activists, and they start attacking everyone."
Article Summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke reacted with "????????????" on X to the CJI judgement. Opposition leader Somnath Bharti said the issue raised larger concerns about access to justice. The CJP's sit-in at Jantar Mantar entered its second month on Wednesday.

Article Title: J&K: Terrorists kill Amarnath Yatra security personnel in Anantnag attack
Article Text:
Suspected terrorists target police party in Anantnag, policeman injured

Poll Vote & Share your view How do you feel about the current security situation in Jammu and Kashmir? Very Concerned Somewhat Concerned Not Concerned Indifferent 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: A police personnel was killed after terrorists attacked a police party in Jammu and Kashmir 's Anantnag town on Wednesday, officials told news agency PTI.The attack took place around 12.30 pm near Lal Chowk in Anantnag town, when the assailants fired at the police team, officials said.Head Constable Ashiq Hussain, who was deployed on Amarnath Yatra security duty, sustained critical injuries in the attack and was rushed to the Government Medical College (GMC) Hospital in Anantnag, officials said.Doctors declared him dead on arrival, they added.Following the attack, security forces cordoned off the area and launched a search operation to trace the assailants.Meanwhile, The Resistance Front (TRF), a proxy outfit of the Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), claimed responsibility for the attack through a post on social media, according to officials.Further details are awaited.In a separate incident, an Army personnel died after accidentally falling from a height near the Line of Control (LoC) in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district on Wednesday, officials said.The deceased, identified as Rifleman Sukhpreet Singh (27), was on operational duty at the forward Mahadev post in the Sunderbani sector when he reportedly lost his balance and fell from a height, resulting in fatal injuries, officials said.His body was shifted to a hospital for post-mortem examination. Police have initiated inquest proceedings, officials added.Following the completion of legal formalities, the soldier's mortal remains will be taken to his native place in Punjab, where he will be accorded the last rites.
Article Summary: Head Constable Ashiq Hussain, who was deployed on Amarnath Yatra security duty, sustained critical injuries. The Resistance Front (TRF), a proxy outfit of the Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), claimed responsibility for the attack. In a separate incident, an Army personnel died after accidentally falling from a height near the Line of Control (LoC)

Article Title: 'Don't waste time': SC nixes plea against police lathicharge on Jantar Mantar protesters
Article Text: NEW DELHI: A Supreme Court bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana Wednesday declined to accept an advocate's suggestion that it take suo motu cognisance of the police action against protesters at Jantar Mantar.The bench said the advocate shouldn't waste his own as well as judicial time on issues not before the court. When the advocate said he had video footage of the violence and police lathicharge, and would file it for viewing by the court, the bench replied, "We do not want to watch videos during judicial work."Meanwhile, Supreme Court Bar Association led by senior advocate Vikas Singh passed a resolution condemning the “brutal lathicharge on innocent students”.“The use of excessive and disproportionate force against peaceful students and members of bar is deeply disturbing and wholly unacceptable in a democratic society,” said the resolution, passed 600 members of SCBA, without mentioning stone pelting and damage to public property by protesters.It sought an “immediate, impartial and time-bound inquiry into the incident, identify those responsible for excessive use of force” for appropriate action in accordance with law and measures to prevent recurrence of such incidents.
Article Summary: The bench said the advocate shouldn't waste his own as well as judicial time on issues not before the court. Supreme Court Bar Association led by senior advocate Vikas Singh passed a resolution condemning the “brutal lathicharge on innocent students”

Article Title: EAM talks of trade with Rubio, sailors with Sergey Lavrov
Article Text:
Jaishankar meets US Secretary of State Marco Rubio

S Jaishankar had a meeting with his Marco Rubio on the sidelines of Asean in Manila, focusing on issues related to trade and tariffs along with the critical minerals partnership. According to a US readout, they agreed on the importance of finalising the interim trade deal, which is "almost complete". They also discussed finalising key defence agreements to fulfil commitments made between President Donald Trump and PM Narendra Modi. "Our relationship with India is vitally important," said Rubio on X.The US ambassador to India said Rubio may visit this year. Jaishankar said the meet focused on priority areas for India-US Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership.In a meeting with his Russia counterpart, Sergey Lavrov, Jaishankar reiterated India's strong concerns about the safety of Indian seafarers. He said they also discussed trade, energy and science.
Article Summary: Jaishankar meets US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on the sidelines of Asean in Manila. They agreed on the importance of finalising the interim trade deal, which is "almost complete" They also discussed finalising key defence agreements to fulfil commitments made between President Donald Trump and PM Narendra Modi.

Article Title: Open to talks with protesters, says govt interlocutor
Article Text:
Health minister J P Nadda, govt’s interlocutor with CJP emphasised govt’s willingness to hold discussions

NEW DELHI: Health minister J P Nadda , govt's interlocutor with Cockroach Janata Party, emphasised govt's willingness to hold discussions with protestors while sidestepping their demand, made by opposition parties too, for the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, asserting the Centre will respond to every charge in debate in Parliament.Addressing a presser, Nadda accused Rahul Gandhi of seeking political mileage over the issue of the NEET-UG paper leak and being selective in his outrage, as the minister listed out numerous cases of leaks in exams held by states where Congress or other opposition parties have been in office.Asked if the demand for Pradhan's resignation has been discussed within govt, he said, "Nahi, is tarike se nahi hota (no, it does not work like this)... We are willing for discussion. It will be in Parliament. They put across their views. Govt will answer."Defence minister Rajnath Singh accused the opposition of trying to use the youth as a political tool for its selfish interests. Opposition netas were stoking a "manufactured sense of anger" to mislead them over the paper leak issue, he said. Nadda asked opposition parties to engage with govt to find a permanent solution to the menace, insisting that it is a serious issue which should not be politicised.With the opposition calling for Pradhan's resignation, he said it should not change goalpost, a reference to govt's claim that Rahul's initial demand was for a discussion. Nadda said he had responded to CJP's demands and was willing for more engagement. No talks were held Wednesday, he added.Rajnath expressed concern over the ongoing protest at Jantar Mantar, now into its second month and drawing a sustained crowd, and emphasised govt's commitment to fulfilling the aspirations of the youth. Slamming the opposition, which has sensed an opening to put the governing alliance on the defensive, he said it was making a wrong attempt to settle issues on the street instead of Parliament.
Article Summary: Health minister J P Nadda accused Rahul Gandhi of seeking political mileage over the issue of the NEET-UG paper leak. Defence minister Rajnath Singh accused the opposition of trying to use the youth as a political tool for its selfish interests.

Article Title: Couple dead, 4 hurt as boulder falls on tempo on Jammu-Srinagar NH in Ramban
Article Text:
Jammu-Srinagar National Highway (NH-44)closed due to landslides, mudslides, and shooting stones

JAMMU: A couple died and four others were injured after a massive boulder fell on a tempo on Jammu-Srinagar national highway (NH-44) near Gangroo in Ramban district Wednesday. Meanwhile, J&K DyCM Surinder Chowdhary and NC Anantnag-Rajouri MP Mian Altaf Ahmad narrowly escaped separate landslides on Jammu-Poonch highway, even as a multi-agency search operation to trace seven missing people in Murrah, Chang Nallah in Poonch’s Surankote entered the fourth day.Officials said the tempo was heading from Srinagar to Doda on NH-44 when a boulder rolled down the hill and struck it. Doda residents Nazir Ahmed and his wife Shakeela Begum died. Police, rescue teams, and local authorities rushed to the spot and moved the bodies, as well as the injured individuals, to Ramsoo PHC.NC MP Ahmed was touring flood-hit areas in Poonch to assess the extent of damage caused by flash floods and incessant rains, when his vehicle got stuck in an active landslide on Jammu-Poonch highway. He escaped unhurt. DyCM Choudhary’s vehicle was hit by a separate landslide on the same highway near Chingus area. Choudhary escaped unhurt, but his vehicle was damaged.Vehicular movement on Jammu-Srinagar NH was suspended following multiple landslides as relentless rainfall battered Jammu region for the fourth day, triggering landslides, mudslides and shooting stones at nearly a dozen places between Udhampur and Banihal.IMD predicted moderate to heavy rains till Thursday evening. “Commuters are advised not to undertake any journey on the highway until the weather improves, and the road is declared safe for traffic,” an official said, adding that clearance agencies were removing debris.Rajouri Police issued an advisory, stating that water levels in rivers had begun to rise again. “Everyone is requested to vacate riverbanks and vulnerable areas immediately and stay alert,” it said.All major pilgrimages — including Amarnath Yatra, Vaishno Devi Yatra, and Machail Mata yatra — have remained suspended in J&K since July 19.
Article Summary: A massive boulder fell on a tempo on Jammu-Srinagar national highway (NH-44) near Gangroo in Ramban district Wednesday. Doda residents Nazir Ahmed and his wife Shakeela Begum died. Meanwhile, J&K DyCM Surinder Chowdhary and NC Anantnag-Rajouri MP Mian Altaf Ahmad narrowly escaped separate landslides.

Article Title: Terrorist handler arrested in J&K’s Kishtwar
Article Text:
Naik was functioning as the main handler of the Saifullah group in Chatroo, said the police

JAMMU: J&K Police late Tuesday arrested Mohammed Rafiq Naik, a key facilitator accused of providing logistical and operational support to foreign terrorists operating in Chatroo area in Kishtwar district.“Naik was functioning as the main handler of the Saifullah group in Chatroo area and allegedly provided active assistance to a group of foreign terrorists operating in the region,” SSP Kishtwar Naresh Singh said, adding that efforts were underway to unearth the full extent of his role in the network and trace his links with the foreign terrorist outfit.The arrest, which police termed a major success in the ongoing crackdown against terrorism, came during investigations into an FIR registered at Chatroo PS under relevant sections of the BNS, UAPA and Arms Act. Sustained probe and targeted operations led cops to Naik, a resident of Dwather in Singhpur, for his alleged role in facilitating terrorist activities, SSP Singh said. “Investigation is continuing to identify and dismantle the entire support network,” he added.Earlier, on June 19, Kishtwar Police dismantled a terror support network by arresting two overground workers, including a forest department employee, who allegedly provided support to local terrorists in Dachan and facilitated their subversive acts. On May 13, Kishtwar Police arrested a teacher in the school education department for providing aid to foreign terrorists at a hideout in Singhpora, Chatroo.
Article Summary: Mohammed Rafiq Naik is accused of providing logistical and operational support to foreign terrorists operating in Kishtwar district. Naik was functioning as the main handler of the Saifullah group in Chatroo, said the police. The arrest came during investigations into an FIR registered at Chatroo PS.

Article Title: Soldier slips to death in J&K’s Rajouri
Article Text:
The deceased, Rifleman Sukhpreet Singh, was a native of Punjab (Representative image)

JAMMU: A 27-year-old Army jawan died after accidentally slipping from a height near the Line of Control (LoC) in J&K’s Rajouri district on Wednesday.The deceased, Rifleman Sukhpreet Singh, was a native of Punjab and posted with JAK Rifles. He was on duty at Mahadev forward post in Sunderbani sector when he suddenly lost his balance and fell from a height, officials said.The body was shifted to a hospital for post-mortem. Police launched inquest proceedings. The remains will be taken to his native place for the last rites, officials said.In a similar tragedy last month, an Army officer died after slipping from a steep mountain ridge and plunging into a deep gorge on June 6 during Operation Sheruwali in Rajouri district. Lt Beereshwar Goswami was leading a search party along a narrow ridge when he lost footing and fell into the gorge. The anti-terror operation, which was launched in the dense forests of Rajouri’s Dorimal-Gambhir-Mughlan belt on May 23 to track two-three Pakistani ultras, is still underway.
Article Summary: The deceased, Rifleman Sukhpreet Singh, was a native of Punjab and posted with JAK Rifles. He was on duty at Mahadev forward post in Sunderbani sector when he suddenly lost his balance and fell from a height.

Article Title: Drone-assisted cross-border drug smuggling surges since 2021, Punjab worst hit: Government
Article Text:
Cross-border drug trafficking has risen exponentially since 2021, with Punjab being the most affected state (representative image)

NEW DELHI: The home ministry on Wednesday said drone-assisted, cross-border drug trafficking has risen exponentially since 2021, with Punjab being the most affected state, accounting for 298 of the 305 countrywide drone-related drug trafficking incidents last year.Replying to a question in the Rajya Sabha, junior home minister Nityanand Rai said nearly 460 kg of narcotic drugs, including 448 kg of heroin, 9 kg of methamphetamine and 3 kg of opium was seized in actions against drone-assisted trafficking.“Trends of cross border narcotics infiltration reveal that drug trafficking syndicates operating across the border have increasingly been employing sophisticated methods such as drones, GPS-enabled consignments, encrypted communications and local receiver networks to infiltrate heroin, methamphetamine, arms and ammunition into India,” Rai informed the Rajya Sabha.
Article Summary: Cross-border drug trafficking has risen exponentially since 2021, home ministry says. Punjab is the most affected state, accounting for 298 of the 305 countrywide drone-related drug trafficking incidents last year. Nearly 460 kg of narcotic drugs, including 448 kg of heroin, 9 kg of methamphetamine and 3 kg of opium was seized in actions against drone-assisted trafficking.

Article Title: 'Responsibility of govt & opposition to protect children's future': Centre urges dialogue as protests intensify - top developments
Article Text:
'Responsibility of govt & opposition to protect children's future': Centre urges dialogue as protests intensify

Here are the top developments throughout the day:

Internet services suspended, Kejriwal raises concerns

Nadda challenges Rahul's paper leak claims, urges Parliament debate

Opposition stages 'Black Day' protest in Parliament

Government offers Parliament discussion; opposition refuses without Pradhan's resignation



Rahul Gandhi attacks Centre, demands Pradhan's resignation



Supreme Court declines urgent hearing



Delhi high court seeks replies, orders CCTV footage preserved

CJP protests continue; Centre deploys more CRPF companies

CJP says it is ready for talks

Opposition MPs blocked from meeting Sonam Wangchuk

Wangchuk sets conditions for ending hunger strike

Wangchuk releases fresh video message

NEW DELHI: A political showdown over the alleged NEET paper leak and police action against student protesters intensified on Wednesday, with Parliament witnessing repeated disruptions, the Opposition staging a 'Black Day' protest, Rahul Gandhi renewing his attack on the Centre and activist Sonam Wangchuk extending his indefinite hunger strike.The Centre maintained that it was ready for a discussion on the issue in Parliament, but the opposition remained firm on its demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.Hitting back at Rahul Gandhi's allegations, Union minister JP Nadda said the government would investigate his claims of around 150 paper leaks and urged the opposition to stop ‘changing the goalpost’ and participate in a parliamentary debate to find a lasting solution.Meanwhile, the Delhi high court sought responses over allegations of police brutality during the students' protest, while the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continued its agitation at Delhi's Jantar Mantar.At least five police personnel were reportedly injured amid fresh clashes at Jantar Mantar as protesters allegedly indulged in stone-pelting. Police responded with tear gas shells and a lathi-charge as tensions spiralled at the protest site.The violence came days after clashes during the CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march.As protests continued in Delhi, Congress general secretary (organisation) KC Venugopal alleged that internet services around Jantar Mantar had been suspended, accusing the Centre of trying to silence protesting students and conceal alleged police excesses. In a post on X, he claimed the move was a direct assault on freedom of speech and warned the government against taking any further action to suppress the agitation.The Delhi Police, meanwhile, said that around 8.30 pm, some miscreants pelted stones and bottles at police personnel near Tolstoy Marg in Connaught Place, injuring ACP Vivek Bhagat. He was taken to RML Hospital by senior officers for treatment. Police said the situation remained tense for a brief period before returning to normal.AAP national convener Arvind Kejriwal also appealed to the Centre not to crack down on students participating in the protests. In a post on X, he urged the government not to take any step against children demonstrating peacefully, warning against any such action.JP Nadda hit back at Rahul Gandhi over his claim that around 150 paper leaks had taken place in the country, saying the matter would be investigated and the government would place the facts before the nation.Referring to Rahul Gandhi's press conference, Nadda said, "Leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi gave a presentation and discussed approximately 150 paper leaks. This is a matter of investigation, and the government, being a responsible government, will undoubtedly investigate and present the truth to the public and the nation."Reiterating the Centre's stand, Nadda said the government was ready for a discussion on the alleged NEET paper leak in Parliament under any format and for any duration decided by the Opposition."We have wanted this issue to be discussed in Parliament. Whether it is a short-duration discussion or a long-duration discussion, you decide how much time you want. The government is ready for discussion, and the Opposition should not keep changing the goalpost," he said.Calling Parliament the appropriate forum to address the issue, Nadda urged all parties to work together for a long-term solution."Children are ours, yours, and the future of the country. It is the responsibility of both the government and the Opposition to protect their future. We should discuss every aspect and come up with a concrete policy," he said.Leader of opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi, along with INDIA bloc MPs, arrived in Parliament dressed in black to
