mark a ‘Black Day’ over the alleged NEET irregularities and the police action against protesting students.Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav, Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra, JMM MP Mahua Maji and several other opposition leaders gathered at Makar Dwar, raising slogans demanding justice for students and the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Amid escalating protests, Parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju said the government was ready to debate the NEET issue in Parliament."The government is ready to hold a discussion on the NEET paper leak issue," Rijiju said, adding that an all-party meeting had been proposed to finalise the date, rule and duration of the debate."There should be a constructive discussion for the future of the youth of this country," he said.However, the opposition maintained that no discussion could take place unless Dharmendra Pradhan resigned.In the Rajya Sabha, Kharge reiterated that the minister must step down before any debate begins, while insisting on a discussion under Rule 267.Leader of the House JP Nadda accused the INDIA bloc of displaying ‘irresponsible behaviour’ and damaging Parliament's dignity.Addressing a press conference, Rahul Gandhi accused the government of destroying India's education system and backed students' demands in full.He claimed the country's education system had become ‘a rigged system’ and alleged that there had been 152 paper leaks in the last decade without a single conviction."The thing that is very clear to everybody is that our education system, which was known to be one of the best education systems in the world, is today a rigged system," he said.He alleged that students were being forced to repeatedly prepare for examinations because of leaks."What we do know is that 152 paper leaks have taken place over the last decade. So, approximately one a month."Rahul also criticised police action against protesters."The question is, why is this happening to our students? What exactly have our students done?... They're protesting peacefully."Calling students' demands ‘100 per cent legitimate’, he said: "Dharmendra Pradhan, who has failed as an education minister and is known to be corrupt, should be relieved of his duties."The Supreme Court declined an urgent hearing on a petition alleging police brutality during Monday's protest.When the petitioner's counsel referred to video evidence, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant remarked: "We are not interested in videos, we don't have time to watch."The petition concerns police action during the CJP's ‘Sansad Chalo’ march, where protesters alleged excessive force by security personnel.The Delhi high court sought responses from the Centre, Delhi Police and the National Human Rights Commission on petitions alleging excessive force during the July 20 protest.A bench headed by Chief Justice DK Upadhyaya directed authorities to preserve CCTV footage, body camera recordings and all relevant electronic records.The court listed the matter for September 11.Petitioners alleged that police and the Rapid Action Force used disproportionate force against peaceful protesters, including women and children.Senior advocate N Hariharan claimed more than 90 students were injured and sought a judicial inquiry and an independent SIT probe.Additional Solicitor General SV Raju argued that the petitions relied on social media posts and maintained that protesters had turned violent, resulting in injuries to police personnel and damage to government vehicles.CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke criticised the government's decision to deploy 20 additional CRPF companies in Delhi."What is the government planning to do? Was the students' blood spilled by the Delhi Police and RAF on 20 July not enough?" he posted on X.The additional CRPF companies are being airlifted from West Bengal to strengthen security amid the ongoing protests and Parliament session.The CJP claimed thousands continued to gather at Jantar Mantar as the agitation entered its second month.The organisation reiterated that its protest would continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.Dipke also clarified that the CJP had not called for protests anywhere other than Jantar Mantar and warned against "goons" attempting to create disturbances elsewhere.The CJP said it was open to discussions with the government but insisted any talks must take place at Jantar Mantar rather than at a minister's residence.Chief spokesperson Saurav Das said police had conveyed that Union minister JP Nadda wanted to meet them."We refused to go to anyone's house or office. The people's court is here," he said.A delegation of opposition MPs was stopped by police from meeting activist Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram.The MPs handed over a letter, signed by around 55 lawmakers, to Wangchuk's wife Gitanjali Angmo urging him to end his fast.AAP MP Sanjay Singh accused the Centre of ‘tyranny’, alleging that Union ministers were allowed to meet Wangchuk while opposition MPs were denied access.“For the first time in history, you've heard that police are preventing people from meeting a hospitalized patient. We've come to meet Sonam Wangchuk, who is hospitalized, and why? 55 five MPs have signed this letter and after signing it, we've brought a letter requesting you to break his hunger strike. Do these people want to kill Sonam Wangchuk? This letter states that you should break your fast. Considering your health and the importance of your life, you should not continue your fast. Sonam Wangchuk had said that if the country's MPs reassure us, he can break his fast. We're not allowed to deliver this letter, not allowed to meet him... JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him and so many MPs from the opposition have come yet they will not let them meet him,” he said.Wangchuk, who has completed 25 days of his indefinite fast, said he would end it only if the government assured that no student protester would face legal action.In a letter to Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, he thanked them for visiting him but sought an unequivocal assurance."I respectfully request an unequivocal assurance from the Government that none of the young protesters will face any punitive or retaliatory legal action."He said if such an assurance was given, he would end his fast immediately.His wife, Gitanjali Angmo, later said Wangchuk had decided to continue the fast until such assurances were received.“Sonam Wangchuk has decided to break his fast after receiving assurances and letters from these MPs. But he also needs assurances from the Government of India that no injustice will be done to the protesters. He also needs assurances from the government that they won't be physically assaulted tonight. Sonam has written this letter to Jitendra ji and Nadda ji, saying that he needs assurances that nothing wrong will be done to the children. No FIR will be filed against them, there will be no undue force against them... We are waiting for another assurance from the Government of India. Sonam has also tweeted today and has also written a letter to them that he wants assurance about the children because if we are doing this entire movement for the children and their future, then it is our responsibility not to cause any harm to them. Sonam has decided to extend his hunger strike indefinitely,” she said.In a video message from hospital, Wangchuk said he had lost around 11 kilograms during his hunger strike but remained determined.He praised students for maintaining restraint despite police action."I want to salute all the students who conducted themselves with remarkable restraint."He reiterated that he wanted to end the fast but would do so only after receiving assurances that students would not face police action, FIRs or legal harassment."If I receive that assurance soon... I will end my hunger strike today itself. But if no such assurance is given, then unfortunately I will have to continue my fast."
Article Summary: Congress general secretary (organisation) KC Venugopal alleged that internet services around Jantar Mantar had been suspended. AAP national convener Arvind Kejriwal also appealed to the Centre not to crack down on students participating in the protests. Delhi high court sought responses over allegations of police brutality during the students' protest. At least five police personnel were reportedly injured amid fresh clashes at the protest site.

Article Title: 'No connection with youth': Goa CM Sawant defends state BJP chief's 'cockroaches' post amid student protests in Delhi
Article Text:
Goa CM Pramod Sawant (File photo/PTI)

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: Goa chief minister and BJP leader Pramod Sawant on Wednesday denied that a social media post by state BJP president Narendra Sawaikar was directed at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protests in Delhi.The CJP, an online satirical platform, was launched after Chief Justice of India Surya Kant's alleged "cockroaches" remark about a section of unemployed youth during a court hearing. The group has been spearheading an indefinite sit-in in Delhi, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET (UG) paper leak and calling for broader reforms to the education system.Sawant's remarks came after Sawaikar shared on X an image of India's map infested with termites and cockroaches, accompanied by the message: "Cockroaches with the termites! Save the nation!"Rejecting the opposition's allegations, Sawant told reporters in Panaji, "The words like 'cockroach' or 'termite' in the post have no connection with the youth. Some people interpret statements according to their political views and have a habit of politicising every issue."Opposition parties had alleged that the post was aimed at the CJP-led sit-in. The Congress on Tuesday demanded that Sawaikar apologise within 24 hours.Meanwhile, hundreds of youths staged a protest in Panaji on Wednesday against Sawaikar's social media post. They gathered at Azad Maidan along with Goa Pradesh Congress Committee (GPCC) president Girish Chodankar, the party's working president Sunil Kawthankar, Goa Pradesh Youth Congress Committee president Archit Naik, Congress spokesperson Amit Palekar and other leaders.Addressing the gathering, Panaji corporator Jack Sukhija said, "Fifteen years is enough time for any government to prove its credibility. The governments at the Centre and in the state have failed, and it is high time that we should send them back home."Former Goa Pradesh Mahila Congress Committee president Pratima Coutinho alleged that the government was trying to divert attention from the agitation in Delhi. "But the protesters' demand is simple. The Union government should sack Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan," she added.Speaking to reporters on the sidelines of the protest, Chodankar urged the Centre to stop "harassing" the country's youth.
Article Summary: Pramod Sawant denies that a social media post by state BJP president Narendra Sawaikar was directed at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protests in Delhi. CJP, an online satirical platform, was launched after Chief Justice of India Surya Kant's alleged "cockroaches" remark about a section of unemployed youth.

Article Title: 'NEET paper leaked thrice in last 5 years': Annamalai lambasts former party BJP
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Former BJP leader K Annamalai on Wednesday criticised the Centre over its handling of the NEET controversy, alleging a lack of accountability despite repeated question paper leaks and questioning the delay in engaging with protesting students.The former Tamil Nadu BJP chief, who now heads the "We the Leaders" movement and is set to launch his own political party, said the government should have initiated talks before the protests escalated."Why was meaningful engagement delayed until the protest had escalated to a flashpoint? What kept you waiting?" Annamalai asked in a statement.Reiterating that protest is a fundamental right in a democracy, he urged the Centre to engage in meaningful dialogue, ensure transparency and take swift action to address students' concerns.Annamalai said he had consistently supported NEET, arguing that it created a uniform admission process and offered students from underprivileged backgrounds a fair opportunity to pursue medical education.However, he said the system had lost credibility after the NEET-UG question paper was allegedly leaked three times in the past five years."Imagine the amount of pressure it puts on students, who had to go through the examination process yet another time when the system that was supposed to bring in uniformity was failing," he said.Alleging there had been "no accountability" for the repeated leaks, Annamalai said the failure of the examination system pushed students onto the streets."I believe strongly that it was that moment that brought in a shift in the minds of the students to take to the streets to cry their hearts out," he said.Stressing that a nation's strength depends on the confidence its youth have in public institutions, Annamalai warned that the education system risks losing credibility if students no longer trust its fairness."If our students lose faith in the fairness and credibility of our education system, we are jeopardising the future of our nation," he said.At the same time, Annamalai urged students and protest organisers to remain focused on their original demands, cautioning that prolonged agitations often attract people with vested interests seeking to divert the movement for political or unrelated agendas."If you remain silent and let these people with vested interests further their agenda, then you are deviating from your core objectives. Please do not let it happen," he said.toi news
Article Summary: K Annamalai criticises the Centre over its handling of the NEET controversy. He alleges a lack of accountability despite repeated question paper leaks. The former Tamil Nadu BJP chief is now heads the "We the Leaders" movement. He is set to launch his own political party.

Article Title: 'Unacceptable in a democratic society': SC bar bodies condemn police action against protesters, lawyers during CJP march
Article Text:
Delhi Police clashed with protesters during the CJP's Parliament march on July 20

NEW DELHI: Two Supreme Court bar bodies on Wednesday condemned the alleged police brutality against students and lawyers during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led July 20 march to Parliament, calling for an impartial inquiry into the "excessive use of force" and demanding accountability from those responsible.The Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA), headed by its president and senior advocate Vikas Singh, in a resolution, alleged that the police action left several students and members of the legal fraternity, including SCBA members, injured.The resolution, issued by SCBA secretary Pragya Baghel, described the use of force against the protesters as "deeply disturbing and wholly unacceptable in a democratic society".The bar body urged the authorities concerned to conduct an immediate, impartial and time-bound inquiry into the incident, identify those responsible for the alleged excessive use of force, and take appropriate action in accordance with the law.It also called for proper medical treatment and assistance for all injured students and members of the legal fraternity, while urging authorities to take steps to prevent similar incidents in the future."The association stands in solidarity with all those injured and reiterates its commitment to protecting constitutional rights, human dignity and the rule of law," the resolution added.In a separate statement, the Supreme Court Advocates-on-Record Association (SCAORA) expressed deep concern over "reports and visuals showing the use of force by Delhi Police against students and young protesters" who had gathered to peacefully raise concerns over the integrity of public examinations and the future of the country's youth.SCAORA, in its resolution, maintained that peaceful dissent was not an act of defiance but an exercise of fundamental freedoms guaranteed under Articles 19(1)(a) and 19(1)(b) of the Constitution. It added that while the State has the authority to maintain public order, any restriction on these freedoms must meet the constitutional requirements of legality, necessity and proportionality.The association called for an independent and impartial examination of allegations of excessive force, immediate medical assistance for those injured, and a more restrained, dialogue-based approach while engaging with peaceful protesters."The strength of our Republic lies not in suppressing dissent but in protecting it within the framework of the Constitution. The true measure of constitutional governance is not how the State responds to agreement, but how it responds to peaceful disagreement," the resolution added.On Monday, thousands of students demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak issue and alleged irregularities in the examination system attempted to march to Parliament but were stopped by police using tear gas and batons.
Article Summary: Two Supreme Court bar bodies have condemned the alleged police brutality against students and lawyers. The bar bodies called for an impartial inquiry into the "excessive use of force" The Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA) alleged that the police action left several students and members of the legal fraternity, including SCBA members, injured.

Article Title: Evening news wrap: Sonam Wangchuk sets condition for ending fast; Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan's exit over NEET row & more
Article Text:
File photo: Activist Sonam Wangchuk and Congress MP Rahul Gandhi

Sonam Wangchuk to end fast only if protesters get legal immunity.

to end fast only if protesters get legal immunity. Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation over NEET row.

demands 's resignation over NEET row. Kalyan Banerjee suspended, calls rebel NCPI MPs "traitors".

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Iran tensions and Trump tariffs rattle markets.

US-Iran tensions deepen with fresh strikes, warnings and regional alerts.

Here are the top stories of the day:

Sonam Wangchuk says he'll end fast only after assurance for protesters

Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan's exit over NEET row

Kalyan Banerjee suspended, calls rebel MPs 'traitors'

Sensex, Nifty slide as global risks spook markets

US-Iran tensions escalate amid strikes, regional alerts

Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has refused to end his hunger strike over the NEET paper leak until the Centre guarantees that no legal action will be taken against participants of the July 20 'Chalo Sansad' march. In a letter to Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, Wangchuk said he wanted to return to teaching but would continue his fast unless the government assured protection for peaceful protesters and addressed key demands on accountability. Read full story Congress leader Rahul Gandhi accused the Centre of turning India's education system into a "rigged system" over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and demanded education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Backing student protests, he alleged 152 paper leaks in the last decade with "zero convictions" and criticised police action against demonstrators.TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee was suspended from the Lok Sabha for the remainder of the Monsoon Session over alleged indecent and misogynistic remarks against women MPs during a heated exchange with rebel NCPI members. After the suspension, Banerjee called the rebel MPs "traitors" and claimed they lacked public support in West Bengal.Equity markets fell sharply, with the Sensex shedding 715 points and the Nifty slipping below 24,000 as escalating US-Iran tensions, surging crude oil prices and fresh tariff threats from US President Donald Trump dampened investor sentiment. Pharma stocks led the losses after Trump's proposed tariffs on imported generic medicines, while a weaker rupee and rising US Treasury yields added to the market pressure, wiping out nearly Rs 4.25 lakh crore in investor wealth.The US and Iran exchanged fresh warnings as secretary of state Marco Rubio said Washington remained open to diplomacy but would not tolerate threats to commercial shipping or the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claimed to have targeted military bases in Jordan, while reports also emerged of explosions in southern Iran and an alleged US strike on Larak Island. Bahrain and Saudi Arabia activated emergency alerts as regional tensions continued to intensify.
Article Summary: US-Iran tensions deepen with fresh strikes, warnings and regional alerts. Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over NEET row. Kalyan Banerjee suspended, calls rebel NCPI MPs "traitors" Sensex, Nifty tumble as Iran tensions and Trump tariffs rattle markets.

Article Title: 'No legal action against CJP protesters': Wangchuk sets condition to end hunger strike
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Wednesday said he will end his hunger strike against the NEET paper leak only after the Centre assures that protesters who participated in the CJP's march will not face any punitive legal action.In a letter addressed to Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, Wangchuk thanked them for visiting him and assuring him that the government would consider his earlier demands."Thank you for visiting me at the hospital last night and for your earnest appeal that I end my fast. During our discussion, you assured me that the government would positively consider the following: Adequate compensation for the families of students who committed suicide following the examination paper leak. A meaningful discussion in Parliament to ensure accountability, including consideration of the resignation of the Hon'ble education minister," Wangchuk said."The Chalo Sansad march on 20 July 2026 remained very peaceful despite the atrocities and disproportionate use of force by the police. The entire nation and the world witnessed their patience and commitment to democratic protest. I sincerely hope that this faith in democratic institutions is not further undermined by any legal cases, harassment or vindictive action against those who participated," he added.Further, Wangchuk said he also wants to end his hunger strike and "live", but not at the cost of the young people for whom this movement began."After your visit, roughly 65 Members of Parliament from across political parties have written to me; some have already come, and others are expected later this evening. They have all urged me to end my fast, reminding me that I still have much work to do in the service of our nation. I agree with them. I want to live. I want to return to my students, to education, and to the work that has defined my life. But I cannot do so at the cost of the very young people for whom this movement began," he said."Therefore, I respectfully request an unequivocal assurance from the Government that none of the young protesters will face any punitive or retaliatory legal action for participating in this movement. Their only 'offence' has been to raise their voice for a fair and accountable education system. If such an assurance is given, I shall end my fast with faith that the Government has heard not only my appeal but also the aspirations of lakhs of young Indians. In the absence of such an assurance, I will be forced to continue my fast indefinitely. I also hope that there will be no further use of excessive police force to repress the movement," he added.
Article Summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk said he will end his hunger strike only after the Centre assures that protesters who participated in the CJP's march will not face any punitive legal action. In a letter addressed to Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, Wangchuk thanked them for visiting him.

Article Title: Delhi HC seeks govt's response on pleas over alleged brutality during CJP march
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Delhi high court on Wednesday issued a notice to the Delhi government on petitions alleging police brutality against students during CJP's march to Parliament on Monday.The court directed the government to preserve all CCTV footage, body camera recordings and other digital records related to the incident, in accordance with the established standard operating procedure.The bench of Chief Justice DK Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia also sought responses from the Centre, Delhi Police and the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) on two public interest litigations (PILs) alleging excessive use of force during the protest. The respondents have been given four weeks to file their replies.The petitions, filed by Umesh Kumar and Anshul Kumar, seek an independent inquiry into the alleged police action during the CJP's "Chalo Sansad" march, in which thousands of students and supporters demanded the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged examination irregularities.Appearing for the petitioners, senior advocates N Hariharan, Vikas Singh and Gopal Sankaranarayanan alleged that Delhi Police and the Rapid Action Force (RAF) used excessive force, including against women and children."Brutality of the largest measure was used against a peaceful agitation... The force used was excessive and uncalled for," Hariharan told the court, urging it to order a judicial inquiry and an independent Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe.He also alleged that more than 90 students were injured and sought directions for registration of FIRs against police personnel allegedly involved in the violence.Senior advocate Vikas Singh claimed that police personnel and individuals in civil clothes assaulted protesters using batons, including some allegedly fitted with nails and electric shock devices. He argued that the protesters, comprising students, lawyers and members of the public, were raising legitimate concerns over examination irregularities.Sankaranarayanan submitted that publicly available videos showed police personnel using force without wearing name tags or uniforms in some cases. Referring to one such video, he alleged that a senior police officer was seen slapping a woman protester.The petitioners also argued that police failed to follow standard crowd-control procedures by using tear gas without adequate warning and sought directions for authorities to produce CCTV footage, drone recordings, body-camera videos and deployment records before the court.Opposing the pleas, additional solicitor general S V Raju, appearing for the Centre and Delhi Police, argued that the petitions were based on social media posts and that any individual alleging assault should seek legal remedies through the appropriate forum.He also claimed that the protest had turned violent, alleging that some protesters resorted to stone-pelting, injured police personnel and damaged police vehicles despite prohibitory orders being in force.The bench, however, observed that the allegations raised in the petitions were not related to an isolated incident and directed the authorities to file their responses, while clarifying that it was not expressing any opinion on the merits of the case.The court has listed the matter for the next hearing on September 11.
Article Summary: Delhi high court issues a notice to the Delhi government on petitions alleging police brutality against students during CJP's march to Parliament on Monday. The court directed the government to preserve all CCTV footage, body camera recordings and other digital records related to the incident. The bench of Chief Justice DK Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia also sought responses from the Centre, Delhi Police and the National Human Rights Commission.

Article Title: 'Students being used as political tools': Rajnath Singh attacks opposition over Delhi protests
Article Text:
Expressing confidence in the country's youth, Singh added that they would not be swayed by what he called attempts to mislead them.

Poll Vote & Share your view Is it beneficial for protests to happen outside of parliamentary discussions? Yes, it raises awareness No, it should be in Parliament 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELIH: Defence minister Rajnath Singh on Wednesday accused the opposition of attempting to use students and young people as "political tools" amid the ongoing protests in Delhi. The defence minister described the demonstrations in the national capital as "a matter of serious concern" and alleged that they were being driven by political interests."The ongoing protests in Delhi are a matter of serious concern. It is extremely unfortunate that some people are attempting to use the students and youth of our country as a political tool to fulfill their political interests," he wrote in a post on X.Rajnath said the Narendra Modi-led government remained committed to addressing the aspirations and concerns of young people."Under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri @narendramodi, our government is fully committed to fulfilling the aspirations of students and youth and to their welfare. It is our responsibility to listen to every concern of our students and resolve it," he said.Expressing confidence in the country's youth, Rajnath added that they would not be swayed by what he called attempts to mislead them."I am fully confident that India's aware, mature, and sensible youth fully understand such deceptive attempts and will choose the path of progress, development, and nation-building."The defence minister also accused opposition leaders of creating a "manufactured Sense of Anger" to influence public opinion, particularly among young people."The Manufactured Sense of Anger by some opposition leaders is merely a failed attempt to confuse and mislead the public, especially our children and youth. We are fully sensitive to the sentiments and concerns of the youth," he wrote.Rajnath also defended the government's approach, noting that Parliament is in session and the Centre has repeatedly expressed its willingness to hold discussions on all issues."Parliament is in session, and the government has also stated its willingness to have meaningful debates on every issue, but the opposition is making an inappropriate attempt to resolve issues not in Parliament but on the streets," he said.Rajnath's remark came as the Rajya Sabha witnessed fresh disruptions on the third day of the Monsoon Session, with the Opposition insisting that Union education minister dharmendra Pradhan resign before any discussion on the alleged NEET paper leak.Parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju said the government was fully prepared to debate the issue but accused the opposition of imposing conditions to avoid a discussion.He said the Centre had been ready to discuss the alleged irregularities in the NEET examination since the session began and had conveyed its willingness to Congress and other opposition parties. Rijiju added that the timing and format of the debate would be decided by the Chair after consultations with all parties.He stressed that the government had a responsibility to explain the steps taken to address concerns over the examination. Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge maintained that no debate would take place until Pradhan stepped down, leading to the House being adjourned until 3 pm.
Article Summary: Defence minister Rajnath Singh accused the opposition of attempting to use students and young people as "political tools" Singh described the demonstrations in the national capital as "a matter of serious concern" The defence minister also accused opposition leaders of creating a "manufactured Sense of Anger"

Article Title: Karachi-bound bus attacked in Balochistan; 3 killed, 5 injured as curfew imposed
Article Text:
In May, Balochistan also witnessed a deadly train bombing targeting security personnel that was claimed by the Baloch Liberation Army (BLA).

Three passengers were killed and five others injured after armed men opened fire on a Karachi-bound passenger bus in Pakistan's restive Balochistan province on Wednesday, prompting authorities to impose an indefinite curfew in the affected area, Dawn reported.According to Dawn, the attack took place in the Garo area of Khud Kocha tehsil in Mastung district. The passenger coach, operated by a private transport company, was travelling from Quetta to Karachi when gunmen positioned on nearby mountains opened indiscriminate fire on the vehicle.Security forces and rescue teams rushed to the scene and shifted the victims to the District Headquarters (DHQ) Hospital in Mastung. The bodies of the three passengers were later handed over to their families after legal formalities, while the injured remained under treatment. Authorities have launched an investigation and are searching for those behind the attack.The violence coincided with a separate explosion that damaged a bridge on the National Highway in the same area. Following the twin incidents, the Balochistan Home Department imposed an indefinite curfew across Khud Kocha sub-tehsil from 9pm on Wednesday.Babar Yousafzai, spokesperson for the Home Department, said the curfew would remain in force until further orders to ensure public safety and maintain law and order. Residents were instructed to remain indoors, with all non-essential movement prohibited. He warned that strict legal action would be taken against anyone violating the curfew and urged people to avoid unnecessary travel.The attack came amid heightened security operations in Balochistan. According to Pakistan's military, security forces killed 10 militants and arrested two female suicide bombers along with their alleged facilitator during intelligence-based operations in Mastung and Surab districts over the past 48 hours.The latest attack adds to a series of militant incidents across Pakistan in recent weeks. Earlier this week, unidentified militants blew up a government middle school in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Bajaur district. In May, Balochistan also witnessed a deadly train bombing targeting security personnel that was claimed by the Baloch Liberation Army (BLA).
Article Summary: Gunmen opened fire on a Karachi-bound passenger bus in Pakistan's restive Balochistan province on Wednesday. The bus was travelling from Quetta to Karachi when gunmen opened indiscriminate fire on the vehicle. Authorities have launched an investigation and are searching for those behind the attack. The violence coincided with a separate explosion that damaged a bridge on the National Highway in the same area.

Article Title: US strikes Iran for 12th night, targets missile, drone and air defence sites
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view What is the best way to reduce tensions between countries? Dialogue and negotiations Stronger security measures 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The US military launched another round of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, marking the 12th consecutive night of attacks, US Central Command (CENTCOM) said. The strikes targeted Iranian military sites, including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites and air defence assets.In a post on X, Centcom added that US forces had struck dozens of Iranian locations on land while resuming a blockade against Iran at sea.“At 10:30 p.m. ET on July 22, U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) forces completed another round of strikes against Iran for the 12th consecutive night,” the post read, adding "U.S. forces struck Iranian military targets including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites, and air defense assets."The command has maintained that the strikes are aimed at reducing Iran’s ability to target shipping in regional waters. The US military said the latest operation lasted five hours.“This month, American forces have targeted dozens of Iranian military sites on land while resuming a blockade against Iran at sea,” CENTCOM said, adding that nine commercial vessels had been redirected and one disabled to prevent ships from entering or departing Iranian ports.The post further added that 50,000 US service members were operating across the Middle East. “More than 50,000 U.S. service members are operating across the Middle East and remain highly vigilant, focused, lethal, and ready,” it said.The latest strikes came as US president Donald Trump said Iran was “getting hit so hard” and described the conflict as a “skirmish”. Speaking in Georgia on Wednesday, Trump claimed Tehran wanted to “make a deal” but was not ready for an agreement.“This skirmish that we have with the Islamic Republic of Iran… They're getting hit so hard, and they want to make a deal,” Trump said. Meanwhile, Iranian foreign minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi warned that any attack on Iran, including its infrastructure, would face a “decisive response”, while other officials also vowed “decisive action”.
Article Summary: US Central Command (CENTCOM) said the latest operation lasted five hours. The strikes targeted Iranian military sites, including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites and air defence assets. The command has maintained that the strikes are aimed at reducing Iran’s ability to target shipping in regional waters.

Article Title: Tear gas, stones, bottles: Video shows late-night clash during CJP protest in Delhi
Article Text:
Protesters allegedly hurled stones and bottles at security personnel deployed near Tolstoy Marg

Poll Vote & Share your view What should be the top priority in addressing the NEET paper leak issue? Take strict action against those responsible Strengthen the exam system 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Fresh violence erupted during the Cockroach Janta Party’s (CJP) protest in the national capital on Wednesday evening, with an assistant commissioner of police (ACP) injured after miscreants allegedly attacked police personnel with stones and bottles in Connaught Place.According to Delhi Police, the incident took place around 8.30pm near Tolstoy Marg in Connaught Place, where protesters allegedly hurled stones and bottles at security personnel deployed in the area.ACP (Connaught Place) Vivek Bhagat sustained injuries in the attack and was rushed to Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital by senior officers.Police said the situation remained tense for a brief period before being brought under control, news agency ANI reported.The protest escalated after police allegedly carried out a lathi charge on students, followed by the use of tear gas. The incident sparked outrage on social media, with many questioning the Delhi Police's actions.Videos of the incident circulating widely on social media show large numbers of protesters at the site. The area was also packed with police personnel deployed before the chaos erupted.The latest violence comes amid the ongoing CJP agitation over the alleged NEET paper leak, which has already witnessed multiple confrontations between protesters and police since the July 20 “Chalo Sansad” march.Hours after the fresh clash, Delhi Police on Thursday said CCTV footage from the July 20 protest corroborated its claim that protesters had attacked security personnel during the demonstration.According to police, the footage shows protesters pelting stones at Delhi Police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel, besides vandalising public property, including smashing the glass windows of shops and a petrol pump.Police said the visuals support its version of events during the July 20 protest, when large-scale clashes broke out as demonstrators attempted to march towards Parliament.Delhi Police has maintained that more than 118 police personnel, including senior officers ranging from special commissioner to assistant commissioner rank as well as several women personnel, were injured during the July 20 violence.Around 60 protesters were also reported injured in the clashes.Special commissioner of police (Law and Order, Zone-I) Devesh Chandra Srivastava on Thursday visited RML Hospital to enquire about the condition of police personnel injured during the protests and interacted with those undergoing treatment.The CJP, however, has rejected the police’s version and accused security personnel of using excessive force against demonstrators.CJP activist Abhijeet Dipke alleged that police brutally assaulted protesters, including women, during the march towards Parliament, claiming male police personnel attacked female protesters.The allegations have not been independently verified, ANI reported.Meanwhile, Union health minister and Leader of the House in the Rajya Sabha JP Nadda said the alleged paper leak issue should not be politicised and reiterated that the government was ready for a detailed discussion in Parliament.“We should move beyond blame and counter-allegations and discuss this. The best medium for discussion is Parliament, and we are ready for that discussion. Let there be a thorough discussion, delving into the details. What are the factors leading to the paper leak? Who are the people involved, and how has the Central Government responded?” Nadda said.The protests, led by activist Sonam Wangchuk and the CJP, are demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET paper leak controversy.The issue has also continued to disrupt Parliament’s Monsoon Session, with the Opposition insisting on Pradhan’s resignation before participating in a discussion, while the government has maintained that it is willing to debate the matter without preconditions.(With agency inputs)
Article Summary: ACP Vivek Bhagat sustained injuries in the attack and was rushed to Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital. Protesters allegedly hurled stones and bottles at security personnel deployed near Tolstoy Marg in Connaught Place. The latest violence comes amid the ongoing CJP agitation over the alleged NEET paper leak.

Article Title: Bikers' group, which suspended rides after PM Narendra Modi's appeal, to resume adventures
Article Text:
The Enfield Tigers Motorcycle Riders Club will resume its thrilling adventures after a three-month hiatus

KOLKATA: After three months off the road, the Enfield Tigers Motorcycle Riders Club, a collective of Royal Enfield owners and riders, is set to restart its breakfast and overnight rides from Aug 7, 2026.The club had voluntarily suspended all organised rides on May 17, responding to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to citizens to conserve oil after the US-Iran war triggered concerns over fuel supplies and prices.Members said the decision, taken during a ride on Bombay Road along NH6, was intended as a show of civic responsibility at a time when the government was urging restraint in fuel consumption.Although the conflict has re-escalated in recent days, several curbs imposed during the crisis, including restrictions on the use of LPG, have now been withdrawn.With fuel-use restrictions lifted, the riders have decided to return to the highways, beginning with a ride to Patratu Valley in Jharkhand and nearby waterfalls."Enfield Tiger Motorcycle Riders Club is just over a year old. On May 17, 2026, while on a breakfast ride along Bombay Road, NH6, we decided to suspend all rides in line with the PM's appeal. We pride at being a responsible riders group and wanted to respond positively to the crisis that the nation faced.Now that the restrictions on fuel usage have been lifted, we are resuming our rides and plan to visit Patratu Valley in Jharkhand and the surrounding waterfalls. Our ride is scheduled to begin on Aug 7, 2026," said Pritam Chatterjee, vice president of the club.Before the suspension, Enfield Tigers had built a regular riding calendar around short breakfast runs and longer overnight journeys.The breakfast rides usually began at dawn, taking members out of the city to highway dhabas before the return leg.The group had also organised longer expeditions to distant destinations such as Ladakh, Arunachal Pradesh and Gujarat, combining motorcycling with travel, camaraderie and road discipline.For members, the resumption marks a return to routine as well as to the community culture that formed around the club in its first year.Organisers said the Aug ride would follow usual safety protocols.
Article Summary: The Enfield Tigers Motorcycle Riders Club will resume its thrilling adventures after a three-month hiatus. The club had voluntarily suspended all organised rides on May 17, responding to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to citizens to conserve oil after the US-Iran war triggered concerns over fuel supplies and prices.

Article Title: Indian-origin woman held in Canada for $60,000 rental scam
Article Text:
Savreet Kaur

An Indian-origin woman has been arrested in Canada after police uncovered a rental scam that left dozens of prospective tenants without homes and out of more than $60,000, CTV News reported.According to Peel Regional Police, the accused allegedly advertised rental properties, including basement apartments, on social media between May 2025 and February 2026. People who responded to the listings signed tenancy agreements and paid deposits covering the first and last month's rent.Police said the fraud came to light only when tenants arrived at the advertised addresses with their belongings, only to discover the properties were unavailable. In several cases, victims had already moved out of their previous homes before realising they had been deceived.Police have identified 17 cases linked to the alleged rental fraud, with total losses exceeding C$60,000. "None of the victims received the promised accommodations or had their money returned," Peel Regional Police said.Savreet Kaur, 27, of Brampton, was arrested on July 13. She faces 38 counts of fraud over C$5,000 and one count of obstructing police. The allegations have not been proven in court. She was held in custody pending a bail hearing.
Article Summary: Savreet Kaur, 27, of Brampton, arrested on July 13. She faces 38 counts of fraud over C$5,000 and one count of obstructing police. Police have identified 17 cases linked to the alleged rental fraud, with total losses exceeding C$60,000.

Article Title: Trump considers military strikes in Mali, US plans to target al-Qaida-linked JNIM
Article Text:
Trump administration weighs action in Mali (ANI image on left)

JNIM ambush kills dozens of soldiers

The Trump administration is considering military strikes in Mali targeting Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM), an al-Qaida-linked militant group, according to current and former US officials cited by The Washington Post.The proposed operation comes amid escalating violence in northern Mali, where JNIM and the separatist Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) have intensified attacks against the Malian military in a bid to seize territory. Since early July, the two groups have been battling government forces despite pursuing different long-term objectives.If approved, Mali would become the eighth country where President Donald Trump has authorised military action since returning to office.The latest escalation follows a JNIM ambush on a Malian military convoy in northern Mali, reportedly leaving at least 50 soldiers dead or captured. The militant group also released videos purportedly showing Malian troops surrendering. Among those reportedly killed or captured were Russian fighters from Russia's Africa Corps, which replaced the Wagner Group in supporting Mali's military.Mali's military said it retaliated with airstrikes that killed around 20 JNIM fighters. The ambush took place between the northern cities of Gao and Anefis, an area where JNIM has been attempting to expand its control.The worsening security situation has raised concerns about the stability of Mali's military-led government, which came to power through coups in 2020 and 2021.The crisis deepened in April when Mali's defence minister was killed in a coordinated attack by JNIM and the FLA. While the FLA seeks an independent homeland in northern Mali, JNIM aims to impose Islamist rule across the country, making their alliance one of tactical convenience rather than shared ideology.
Article Summary: Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) is an al-Qaida-linked militant group. JNIM and the separatist Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) have intensified attacks against the Malian military in a bid to seize territory.

Article Title: Japan sizzles in first 'brutally hot' days of year
Article Text:
Japan swelters in heat

TOKYO: Japan sizzled under sweltering, life-threatening heat Wednesday, with temperatures expected to top 40Celsius in some cities in what govt has newly designated as a "brutally hot day". Authorities urged residents to use air conditioning liberally, hydrate regularly and avoid spending long hours outdoors.Japan has experienced increasingly severe summers in recent years, with the country recording its all-time high temperature of 41.8C last year. Even before this week's extreme heat, 4,580 people were taken to hospitals between July 6 and 12, including seven who later succumbed, according to the data available from the fire and disaster management agency.(This is a Reuters story)
Article Summary: Temperatures expected to top 40C in some cities. Japan has experienced increasingly severe summers in recent years. All-time high temperature of 41.8C recorded last year. 4,580 people taken to hospitals between July 6 and 12, including seven who later succumbed.

Article Title: 'Ban Muslims from office': Laura Loomer calls Zohran Mamdani 'national security risk'
Article Text:
Zohran Mamdani and Laura Loomer (R)

America's far-right activist and Donald Trump loyalist Laura Loomer on Wednesday launched a scathing attack on New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani after he called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as a "war criminal" and said he was "not welcome in New York City."Loomer, who is Jewish, called for a ban on Muslims holding public office in the United States and urged President Donald Trump to reinstate a "Muslim ban.""Ban Muslims from holding office in America," Loomer said.She also appealed directly to MAGA supremo, writing: "We are never going to Make America Safe Again until President Trump goes back to implementing his Muslim ban. This time, make it a Muslim Ban as it was intended to be. Not an Islamic travel ban."Taking to X, Loomer accused Mamdani of sympathising with Iran and Hamas, and claimed, without providing evidence, that he posed a national security risk. "When you allow Muslims to participate in the American political system, you get Twelver Shia Iran regime sympathizers and Hamas lovers like Mamdani governing the largest city in America. It won’t end well."She further claimed that Mamdani would never receive FBI security clearance, saying, "I’m just glad the Trump administration confirmed to me that Mamdani will never be given an FBI clearance."Citing an unnamed source, Loomer wrote, "My senior FBI source told me, 'Senior FBI leadership has reason to believe Mamdani may be having direct communication with Islamic terrorist organizations. He is certainly a national security risk which is why we declined giving him an FBI approved security clearance. He won’t be getting one.'"Loomer added, "I pushed very hard for that to be the case even before Mamdani was elected," and further claimed, "Shocking my FBI source confirmed to me that they allegedly have reason to believe Mamdani is having direct communication with terror orgs."The remarks came after Mamdani intensified his criticism of Netanyahu in a video posted on X on Tuesday, saying the Israeli leader was "not welcome in New York City" and urging the US federal government to enforce the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrant against him.Calling Netanyahu "a war criminal," Mamdani accused him of committing atrocities during Israel's military campaign in Gaza."He is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people," Mamdani said.He further alleged that Netanyahu was responsible "for the maiming of tens of thousands of children as those who survive undergo amputation without anaesthesia. For the targeting of neonatal hospitals and maternity care centres, denying newborns even the chance to live. For the countless people he starved as he blocked food and humanitarian aid from reaching them."Referring to the ICC arrest warrant, Mamdani said: "There is a reason the International Criminal Court issued a warrant for his arrest. As I've said, I agree with the ICC that Benjamin Netanyahu should be arrested and tried for his crimes as I do for anyone else charged by the ICC."
Article Summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as a "war criminal" and said he was "not welcome in New York City" Loomer, who is Jewish, called for a ban on Muslims holding public office in the United States and urged President Donald Trump to reinstate a "Muslim ban"
